properties.title: Amazon River Delta
properties.description: The Amazon River meets the Atlantic Ocean in a vast delta spanning over 100 km. Sediment-rich freshwater plumes extend far into the ocean, visible as brown and green swirls from orbit.

properties.title: Manhattan, New York
properties.description: The island of Manhattan — one of the most densely developed urban landscapes on Earth, framed by the Hudson and East rivers.

properties.title: Eye of the Sahara (Richat Structure)
properties.description: The Richat Structure in Mauritania — a 40 km-diameter geological dome eroded into concentric rings visible from orbit.

properties.title: Great Barrier Reef
properties.description: The world's largest coral reef system stretching over 2,300 km along Australia's northeast coast. Turquoise shallow waters contrast with the deep blue of the Coral Sea.

properties.title: Himalayas — Mount Everest Region
properties.description: The roof of the world seen from orbit. Snow-covered peaks above 8,000 m cast long shadows across glacial valleys.

properties.title: Venice Lagoon, Italy
properties.description: The city of Venice sits within a shallow coastal lagoon in the northern Adriatic Sea. Tidal channels, barrier islands, and the historic city center are visible from orbit.
